meta.title: L319 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Land Rover Discovery 3 L319 engine — TDV6, V6, V8. Real UK costs, the TDV6 crankshaft risk explained, and honest reliability data. Get free quotes from 12 vetted Land Rover specialists today.

@graph.name: Land Rover Discovery 3 L319 Engine Codes
Land Rover Discovery 3 L319 Core Variants

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery 3 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Discovery 3 engine typically costs £2,500–£7,500 supply only [EM-QUOTE], depending on engine — the 276DT TDV6 diesel sits at £5,000–£7,500, while the 4.0 V6 petrol is significantly cheaper at £2,500–£4,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on engine and specialist location.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover Discovery 3 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 448PN 4.4 V8 petrol and the 406PN 4.0 V6 petrol are the most reliable options — both are naturally aspirated, simpler designs with no catastrophic failure modes. The 276DT TDV6 diesel carries this generation's defining risk: crankshaft-bearing failure is a well-documented £5,000+ bill waiting to happen [PROVISIONAL — pending dedicated Engine Page].

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Discovery 3?

acceptedAnswer.text: Crankshaft-bearing failure and oil pump housing failure on the 276DT TDV6 diesel are the two most significant engine problems — both can destroy the engine without warning. The 448PN V8 petrol suffers from coolant crossover pipe leaks, which are cheap to prevent but expensive if ignored. The 406PN V6 is the most robust engine in the range.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Discovery 3 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on which engine you choose. Petrol-powered L319s (4.0 V6 and 4.4 V8) are generally reliable, well-understood designs. The 2.7 TDV6 diesel, however, is high-risk — crankshaft-bearing failure and oil pump issues are common enough that we recommend avoiding the diesel unless you have documented proof of preventative work already completed.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover Discovery 3 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 276DT 2.7 TDV6 diesel is the engine we consistently advise against — catastrophic crankshaft-bearing failure is a well-documented pattern, and a full engine replacement costs £5,000–£7,500 [EM-QUOTE], which frequently exceeds the value of an older Discovery 3. Without documented oil pump or crankshaft work, it's a gamble we wouldn't take.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my Discovery 3 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cold-start knock on the TDV6 diesel is the classic early warning of crankshaft-bearing failure — investigate immediately. Low oil pressure warning lights, rough running, or blue smoke from any engine are also key warning signs. Use our diagnostic tool for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery 3 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £12,000 Discovery 3, a £5,000 TDV6 recon is 42% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £5,000 example, the same recon is 100% — walk away. Our 60% Land Rover scrap threshold is the line: if replacement cost exceeds 60% of the car's value, evaluate scrap first [EM-VERIFIED].

mainEntity.name: How long do Land Rover Discovery 3 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 448PN V8 petrol can exceed 200,000 miles with proper servicing — its only major weakness is coolant crossover pipe leaks, which are preventable. The 406PN V6 is similarly durable. The 276DT TDV6 diesel is limited by its crankshaft-bearing design; addressed proactively with oil analysis and preventative bearing replacement, it can reach 140,000+ miles, but many fail earlier.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the Discovery 3 and Discovery 4?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both share the L319 chassis code, but the Discovery 4 (2009–2016) received significant mechanical updates including the 3.0 SDV6 diesel, a revised 2.7 TDV6, a 5.0 V8 petrol, and a facelifted interior. Costs and reliability profiles differ — see the Discovery 4 Generation Page for specifics.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Discovery 3 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; the engine code is also stamped on the engine block (visible from below on the TDV6, and from above on the V6/V8 petrols). Tell us your variant (TDV6, V6, or V8) and we'll identify it — 12 vetted Land Rover specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: Land Rover Discovery 3 L319 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the Discovery 3 L319 — from the 2.7-litre TDV6 diesel to the 4.4-litre V8 petrol. Real UK costs and honest failure data for the crankshaft-bearing risk that defines this generation. Backed by 120+ real UK L319 enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Land Rover Discovery 3 L319 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the Discovery 3 L319 specifically — three engines, one defining diesel risk.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: 276DT TDV6: 95% confidence, derived from the EM_Proprietary_Data Known Failure Patterns table and Model Ownership Decision Table — crankshaft bearing failure is a well-documented catastrophic risk. 406PN V6 petrol and 448PN V8 petrol: 95% confidence, derived from workshop consensus and specialist sources — both are more robust than the TDV6 diesel. All ratings are [PROVISIONAL] pending dedicated Engine Pages for each family.

overview.intro: The L319 is the third-generation Discovery, launched in April 2005 as a ground-up redesign of the iconic off-roader. Built on an Integrated Body Frame (IBF) combining monocoque construction with a ladder-frame chassis, it introduced independent double-wishbone suspension and, on SE/HSE models, cross-linked air suspension. The engine line-up offered three choices: a 2.7-litre TDV6 diesel, a 4.0-litre Cologne V6 petrol, and a 4.4-litre AJ-V8 petrol — but the TDV6's crankshaft-bearing vulnerability defines this generation's ownership story.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Land Rover L319 (Integrated Body Frame) • Predecessor: Discovery 2 (L318) • Successor: Discovery 4 (L319, 2009–2016) • Sibling body styles in this generation: L319 commercial van (2007–2009).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The L319 Discovery 3 generated 120+ real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED], concentrated on the 276DT TDV6 diesel as owners confront the crankshaft-bearing failure risk.

ownershipEconomics.h2: Land Rover Discovery 3 L319 — Ownership Economics

rows.ownershipVerdict: High Risk — catastrophic failure common; avoid without history.

economicsRule.title: The L319 rule
economicsRule.text: A £5,000–£7,500 TDV6 recon against a £4,000–£14,000 Discovery 3 sits at 35–180% of the car's value【EM-QUOTE】. On a sub-£6,000 example, that brushes our 60% Land Rover scrap threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £12,000+ example, the same recon drops to 40–60% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first.

keyTakeaways.question: Which L319 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 406PN V6 petrol — simple Cologne V6, cheap parts, no catastrophic failure mode.

keyTakeaways.question: Which L319 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 276DT TDV6 — crankshaft bearing failure is a £5,000+ bill waiting to happen.

keyTakeaways.question: Which L319 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: 448PN V8 petrol — thirsty but the most durable engine in the range.

problems.title: 276DT TDV6 Crankshaft Bearing Failure
problems.rootCause: The tin-aluminium coating on the crankshaft bearings fatigues over time, leading to bearing spin and catastrophic engine failure. Cold-start knock is the classic early warning sign. Oil pump housing failure can also occur, causing sudden oil pressure loss and rapid engine death.

tieredCosts.work: Oil analysis, sump inspection, bearing replacement if caught early

problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold-start knock or low oil pressure, get the engine inspected immediately — a £1,200–£2,500 preventative bottom-end rebuild is dramatically cheaper than the £5,000–£7,500 replacement that follows if ignored. On an L319 worth under £6,000, this decision alone determines whether the car stays economically viable.

cta.label: Compare 276DT TDV6 engine prices for your Discovery 3 →

problems.title: 276DT TDV6 Oil Pump Housing Failure
problems.rootCause: The oil pump housing casing can crack, dislodging the timing belt and causing sudden oil pressure loss. This often occurs without warning and can destroy the engine in seconds — it is considered a design weakness in the 276DT.

tieredCosts.work: Oil pump housing inspection and replacement

problems.recommendation: Have the oil pump housing inspected during every major service — a £900–£1,800 preventative replacement is far cheaper than the £5,000+ engine replacement that follows a catastrophic failure. This is cheap insurance on an otherwise capable off-roader.

cta.label: Compare 276DT TDV6 engine prices for your Discovery 3 →

problems.title: 448PN AJ-V8 Coolant Crossover Pipe Leaks
problems.rootCause: The plastic coolant crossover pipes at the front of the V8 engine become brittle with age and heat cycling, leading to coolant leaks and potential overheating. This is the most common failure on the otherwise robust AJ-V8.

tieredCosts.work: Engine replacement if overheating caused head gasket failure

problems.recommendation: Replace the coolant crossover pipes as preventative maintenance at 80,000 miles or 10 years — a £500–£900 specialist job is trivial against the £4,000+ engine replacement that follows an ignored coolant leak. This is the single most important maintenance item on the 4.4 V8.

cta.label: Compare 448PN V8 engine prices for your Discovery 3 →

replacementCosts.h2: L319 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the EM_Proprietary_Data baseline figures — L319 Discovery 3 shares the same engine-bay access as the Discovery 4 (also L319), so costs are inherited directly per the Authority Model, with no divergence note required.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are Discovery 3 L319 only. Discovery 4 (also L319, 2009–2016) shares the same chassis code but received different engines including the 3.0 SDV6, 5.0 V8, and revised 2.7 TDV6 — those are covered on the Discovery 4 Generation Page. The L319 commercial van (2007–2009) shares the 2.7 TDV6 engine.
